::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
pacman::p_load(sf, sfdep, tmap, tidyverse)
```
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
hunan <- st_read(dsn = "data/geospatial", 
                 layer = "Hunan")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
Reading layer `Hunan' from data source 
  `/Users/yangyayong/Downloads/学校文件/smu文件/Term 3/G/yyyirene/ISSS626-GAA/In-class_EX/In-class_EX06/data/geospatial' 
  using driver `ESRI Shapefile'
Simple feature collection with 88 features and 7 fields
Geometry type: POLYGON
Dimension:     XY
Bounding box:  xmin: 108.7831 ymin: 24.6342 xmax: 114.2544 ymax: 30.12812
Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84
```


:::
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
GDPPC <- read_csv("data/aspatial/Hunan_GDPPC.csv")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
Rows: 1496 Columns: 3
── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Delimiter: ","
chr (1): County
dbl (2): Year, GDPPC

ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
```


:::
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Create a space-time cube object  #each time have a layer
GDPPC_st <- spacetime(GDPPC, hunan,
                       .loc_col = "County",#space indicator
                       .time_col = "Year")  #time  integer 
```
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Check if it is a space-time cube object
is_spacetime_cube(GDPPC_st)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] TRUE
```


:::
:::


inverse distance weights(g\* should be included itself)


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Compute Gi* statistic
# Get neighbors and derive spatial weights
GDPPC_nb <- GDPPC_st %>%
  activate("geometry") %>%  #must have this to compute 
  mutate(nb = include_self(
           st_contiguity(geometry)),#how many neighbour
         wt = st_inverse_distance(nb, 
                                  geometry, 
                                  scale = 1,
                                  alpha = 1),
  .before = 1) %>% #infront of the table
  set_nbs("nb") %>% #
  set_wts("wt")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
! Polygon provided. Using point on surface.
```


:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
Warning: There was 1 warning in `stopifnot()`.
ℹ In argument: `wt = st_inverse_distance(nb, geometry, scale = 1, alpha = 1)`.
Caused by warning in `st_point_on_surface.sfc()`:
! st_point_on_surface may not give correct results for longitude/latitude data
```


:::
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
gi_stars <- GDPPC_nb %>%
  group_by(Year) %>%
  mutate(gi_star = local_gstar_perm(
    GDPPC, nb, wt)) %>%
  tidyr::unnest(gi_star)
```
:::


this have trend ,moran may not have trend


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
cbg <- gi_stars %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(County == "Changsha") %>%
  select(County, Year, gi_star)
```
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(data = cbg, aes(x = Year, y = gi_star)) +
  geom_line() +
  theme_light()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](In-class_EX06_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-9-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
cbg %>%
  summarise(mk = list(
    unclass(
      kendall::Mannkendall(gi_star)
    )
  )) %>%
  tidyr::unnest_wider(mk)
```
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ehsa <- gi_stars %>%
  group_by(County) %>%
  summarise(mk = list(
    unclass(
      Kendall::MannKendall(gi_star)
    )
  )) %>%
  tidyr::unnest_wider(mk)

head(ehsa)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 6 × 6
  County        tau        sl     S     D  varS
  <chr>       <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>

```


:::
:::

::: {.cell}

:::


Perfoming the emerging hotspot analysis


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ehsa<-emerging_hotspot_analysis(
  x=GDPPC_st,
  .var = "GDPPC",
  K=1,
  nism=99
)
```
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(data=ehsa,
       aes(x=classification))+
  geom_bar
```
:::


not all is statistic significant. to use the p- value to do it

EHSA


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
hunan_ehsa <- hunan %>%
  left_join(ehsa, by = join_by(County == location))
```
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ehsa_sig <- hunan_ehsa %>%
  filter(p_value < 0.05)

tmap_mode("plot")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
tmap mode set to plotting
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
tm_shape(hunan_ehsa) +
  tm_polygons(alpha = 0.5) +
  tm_borders(alpha = 0.5) +
tm_shape(ehsa_sig) +
  tm_fill("classification") +
  tm_borders(alpha = 0.4)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
Warning: One tm layer group has duplicated layer types, which are omitted. To
draw multiple layers of the same type, use multiple layer groups (i.e. specify
tm_shape prior to each of them).
```


:::
